resources.string: Voice settings load from the server — set the URL and key above, then Test connection.
Nothing listable at that base URL — type a model ID
No hands-free conversations yet — say “Hey Axon” to start one.
Delete this conversation? This can\'t be undone.
Enroll your voice so "Hey Axon" and mid-reply interruptions only count if they sound like you — filters out other people, background talk, coughs, and other noise.
Recording %1$d of %2$d — speak naturally for a few seconds…
Voice ID enrolled ✓ — only your voice wakes Axon or confirms a barge-in
Not enrolled — wake word and barge-in use sound/keyword match only
Enrollment failed — try again somewhere quieter

How closely a mid-reply interruption must match your enrolled voice before Axon stops to listen. Lower it if Axon ignores your real interruptions; raise it if other voices cut in. Only applies while Voice ID is enrolled.

Fine-tune how Axon hears you interrupt a spoken reply, before the voice match even runs. Changes apply on your next reply. To tune from real numbers, run `adb logcat -s BargeDetector SpeakerVerify` while you try to interrupt. If it gets worse, tap Reset.

How much louder than the reply\'s echo your voice must be to break in. Lower = easier to interrupt (raise the sensitivity if Axon ignores you); higher = fewer accidental triggers but a harder barge-in.

How hard Axon corrects its echo estimate when the reply\'s volume pumps up and down. Off never corrects; stronger stops pumping faster but raises the bar for interrupting. Turn it down if it pumps rarely and you want easier barge-in; up if it pumps often.
How far the reply\'s volume dips while Axon checks whether you\'re interrupting. Lower makes your voice easier to hear over the echo; the dip is more noticeable on a false alarm.
How long Axon holds the reply\'s loudness in memory across the gaps between streamed sentences. Higher reduces volume pumping at sentence boundaries; too high and a finished reply\'s echo lingers.
